nodes.name: SDTM
nodes.description: Study Data Tabulation Model Implementation Guide v3.4

nodes.name: Special Purpose
nodes.description: Domains that do not conform to one of the three general observation classes

nodes.name: Interventions
nodes.description: Data that reflect study treatments, such as drug exposure or procedures

nodes.name: Events
nodes.description: Data that reflect occurrences, such as adverse events or disposition

nodes.name: Findings
nodes.description: Data that reflect observations, such as laboratory tests or vital signs

nodes.name: Trial Design
nodes.description: Domains describing the design of the clinical trial

nodes.name: Demographics
nodes.description: One record per subject. Contains demographic and baseline information.

nodes.name: STUDYID
nodes.label: Study Identifier
nodes.description: Unique identifier for a study

nodes.name: DOMAIN
nodes.label: Domain Abbreviation
nodes.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

nodes.name: USUBJID
nodes.label: Unique Subject Identifier
nodes.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications

nodes.name: SUBJID
nodes.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
nodes.description: Subject identifier within a study

nodes.name: RFSTDTC
nodes.label: Subject Reference Start Date/Time
nodes.description: Reference start date/time for the subject in ISO 8601

nodes.name: RFENDTC
nodes.label: Subject Reference End Date/Time
nodes.description: Reference end date/time for the subject in ISO 8601

nodes.name: RFXSTDTC
nodes.label: Date/Time of First Study Treatment
nodes.description: First date/time of study treatment exposure

nodes.name: RFXENDTC
nodes.label: Date/Time of Last Study Treatment
nodes.description: Last date/time of study treatment exposure

nodes.name: RFICDTC
nodes.label: Date/Time of Informed Consent
nodes.description: Date/time of informed consent in ISO 8601

nodes.name: RFPENDTC
nodes.label: Date/Time of End of Participation
nodes.description: Date/time of the end of a subject's participation in a study

nodes.name: DTHDTC
nodes.label: Date/Time of Death
nodes.description: Date/time of death of the subject in ISO 8601

nodes.name: DTHFL
nodes.label: Subject Death Flag
nodes.description: Indicates the subject died (Y or N)

nodes.name: SITEID
nodes.label: Study Site Identifier
nodes.description: Unique identifier for a site

nodes.name: BRTHDTC
nodes.label: Date/Time of Birth
nodes.description: Date/time of birth

nodes.name: AGE
nodes.label: Age
nodes.description: Age at reference start date

nodes.name: AGEU
nodes.label: Age Units
nodes.description: Units associated with AGE

nodes.name: SEX
nodes.label: Sex
nodes.description: Sex of the subject

nodes.name: RACE
nodes.label: Race
nodes.description: Race of the subject (from a multi-race code list)

nodes.name: ETHNIC
nodes.label: Ethnicity
nodes.description: The ethnicity of the subject

nodes.name: ARMCD
nodes.label: Planned Arm Code
nodes.description: Short description of the planned arm for a subject

nodes.name: ARM
nodes.label: Description of Planned Arm
nodes.description: Name of the arm to which the subject was assigned

nodes.name: ACTARMCD
nodes.label: Actual Arm Code
nodes.description: Code for the actual arm for a subject

nodes.name: ACTARM
nodes.label: Description of Actual Arm
nodes.description: Description of the actual arm for a subject

nodes.name: COUNTRY
nodes.label: Country
nodes.description: Country of the investigational site (ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3)

nodes.name: DMDTC
nodes.label: Date/Time of Collection
nodes.description: Date/time of demographic data collection

nodes.name: DMDY
nodes.label: Study Day of Collection
nodes.description: Study day of demographic data collection

nodes.name: Adverse Events
nodes.description: One record per adverse event per subject. Contains adverse event data.
nodes.structure: One record per adverse event per subject

nodes.name: STUDYID
nodes.label: Study Identifier
nodes.description: Unique identifier for a study

nodes.name: DOMAIN
nodes.label: Domain Abbreviation
nodes.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

nodes.name: USUBJID
nodes.label: Unique Subject Identifier
nodes.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies

nodes.name: AESEQ
nodes.label: Sequence Number
nodes.description: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records

nodes.name: AETERM
nodes.label: Reported Term for the Adverse Event
nodes.description: Verbatim name of the adverse event as reported by the investigator

nodes.name: AEMODIFY
nodes.label: Modified Reported Term
nodes.description: Modified reported term if different from AETERM

nodes.name: AEDECOD
nodes.label: Dictionary-Derived Term
nodes.description: Dictionary-derived term (MedDRA Preferred Term)

nodes.name: AEBODSYS
nodes.label: Body System or Organ Class
nodes.description: Dictionary-derived (MedDRA System Organ Class)

nodes.name: AESEV
nodes.label: Severity/Intensity
nodes.description: The severity or intensity of the event

nodes.name: AESER
nodes.label: Serious Event
nodes.description: Is the event classified as serious?

nodes.name: AEACN
nodes.label: Action Taken with Study Treatment
nodes.description: Action taken with study treatment as a result of the event

nodes.name: AEREL
nodes.label: Causality
nodes.description: Investigator's opinion of the relationship of the event to treatment

nodes.name: AEOUT
nodes.label: Outcome of Adverse Event
nodes.description: Description of the outcome of the event

nodes.name: AESCAN
nodes.label: Involves Cancer
nodes.description: Is the event a cancer?

nodes.name: AESCONG
nodes.label: Congenital Anomaly or Birth Defect
nodes.description: Is the event a congenital anomaly?

nodes.name: AESDISAB
nodes.label: Persist or Signif Disability/Incapacity
nodes.description: Event results in persistent or significant disability?

nodes.name: AESDTH
nodes.label: Results in Death
nodes.description: Is the event associated with the outcome of death?

nodes.name: AESHOSP
nodes.label: Requires or Prolongs Hospitalization
nodes.description: Event requires or prolongs hospitalization?

nodes.name: AESLIFE
nodes.label: Is Life Threatening
nodes.description: Is the event life threatening?

nodes.name: AESOD
nodes.label: Occurred with Overdose
nodes.description: Event occurred with an overdose?

nodes.name: AESTDTC
nodes.label: Start Date/Time of Adverse Event
nodes.description: Start date/time of the adverse event in ISO 8601

nodes.name: AEENDTC
nodes.label: End Date/Time of Adverse Event
nodes.description: End date/time of the adverse event in ISO 8601

nodes.name: AESTDY
nodes.label: Study Day of Start of Adverse Event
nodes.description: Study day of start of adverse event relative to reference date

nodes.name: AEENDY
nodes.label: Study Day of End of Adverse Event
nodes.description: Study day of end of adverse event relative to reference date

nodes.name: AEENRF
nodes.label: End Relative to Reference Period
nodes.description: Describes the end of the event relative to the reference period

nodes.name: Laboratory Test Results
nodes.description: One record per lab test per time point per subject. Contains laboratory test result data.
nodes.structure: One record per lab test per time point per subject

nodes.name: STUDYID
nodes.label: Study Identifier
nodes.description: Unique identifier for a study

nodes.name: DOMAIN
nodes.label: Domain Abbreviation
nodes.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

nodes.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies

nodes.name: LBSEQ
nodes.label: Sequence Number

nodes.name: LBTESTCD
nodes.label: Lab Test or Examination Short Name

nodes.description: Verbatim name of the test (e.g., Alanine Aminotransferase)

nodes.label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Orig Unit
nodes.description: Lower limit of the reference range in original units

nodes.name: LBORNRHI
nodes.label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Orig Unit
nodes.description: Upper limit of the reference range in original units

nodes.name: LBSTRESC
nodes.label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
nodes.description: Contains the result value for all findings in character format

nodes.label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
nodes.description: Numeric result value in standard units

nodes.name: LBSTRESU
nodes.label: Standard Units
nodes.description: Standardized units for LBSTRESN

nodes.name: LBSTNRLO
nodes.label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Std Units
nodes.description: Lower limit of the reference range in standard units

nodes.name: LBSTNRHI
nodes.label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Std Units
nodes.description: Upper limit of the reference range in standard units

nodes.name: LBNRIND
nodes.label: Reference Range Indicator
nodes.description: Indicates where the value falls relative to the reference range (NORMAL, LOW, HIGH)

nodes.name: LBSPEC
nodes.label: Specimen Type
nodes.description: Type of specimen (e.g., BLOOD, SERUM, URINE, PLASMA)

nodes.name: LBMETHOD
nodes.label: Method of Test or Examination
nodes.description: Method of the laboratory test

nodes.name: LBBLFL
nodes.label: Baseline Flag
nodes.description: Indicator for whether the result is a baseline value

nodes.name: LBFAST
nodes.label: Fasting Status
nodes.description: Was the subject fasting for this specimen collection?

nodes.name: VISITNUM
nodes.label: Visit Number
nodes.description: Numeric version of VISIT

nodes.description: Date/time of specimen collection in ISO 8601

nodes.name: LBDY
nodes.label: Study Day of Specimen Collection
nodes.description: Study day of specimen collection

nodes.description: Numeric visit

nodes.description: Study day of vital signs measurement

edges.description: SDTM tabulation datasets are transformed into ADaM analysis datasets for statistical analysis
CDASH (Clinical Data Acquisition Standards Harmonization) defines collection standards that map to SDTM tabulation domains
ADaM datasets are used to produce Tables, Listings, and Figures for regulatory submissions
Each adverse event in AE links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Each medication record in CM links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Each exposure record in EX links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Each lab result in LB links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Each vital sign measurement in VS links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Concomitant medications may be taken in response to adverse events (CM.CMINDC references AE)
Adverse events may result in changes to study treatment exposure (AEACN: DOSE REDUCED, DRUG WITHDRAWN, etc.)
Laboratory tests are often scheduled relative to treatment exposure to monitor drug effects
Vital signs are often measured relative to dosing events to monitor treatment impact
Abnormal lab values (LBNRIND = HIGH/LOW) may correlate with or trigger adverse events

variables.description: Category for the treatment

variables.name: EXDOSE
variables.label: Dose
variables.description: Amount of study treatment administered

variables.name: EXDOSFRM
variables.label: Dose Form
variables.description: Pharmaceutical dose form of study treatment

variables.name: EXDOSFRQ
variables.label: Dosing Frequency per Interval
variables.description: Frequency of dosing

variables.name: EXDOSU
variables.label: Dose Units
variables.description: Units for EXDOSE

variables.name: EXENDTC
variables.label: End Date/Time of Treatment
variables.description: End date/time of treatment in ISO 8601

variables.name: EXENDY
variables.label: Study Day of End of Treatment
variables.description: Study day of end of treatment

variables.name: EXLOT
variables.label: Lot Number
variables.description: Lot number of study treatment

variables.name: EXROUTE
variables.label: Route of Administration
variables.description: Route of administration

variables.name: EXSEQ
variables.label: Sequence Number
variables.description: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records

variables.name: EXSTDTC
variables.label: Start Date/Time of Treatment
variables.description: Start date/time of treatment in ISO 8601

variables.name: EXSTDY
variables.label: Study Day of Start of Treatment
variables.description: Study day of start of treatment

variables.name: EXTRT
variables.label: Name of Treatment
variables.description: Name of the study drug or treatment administered

variables.name: STUDYID
variables.label: Study Identifier
variables.description: Unique identifier for a study

variables.name: USUBJID
variables.label: Unique Subject Identifier
variables.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies

related_domains.description: Laboratory tests are often scheduled relative to treatment exposure to monitor drug effects
Vital signs are often measured relative to dosing events to monitor treatment impact
Each exposure record in EX links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Adverse events may result in changes to study treatment exposure (AEACN: DOSE REDUCED, DRUG WITHDRAWN, etc.)

LB.name: Laboratory Test Results
LB.description: One record per lab test per time point per subject. Contains laboratory test result data.
LB.structure: One record per lab test per time point per subject

variables.name: DOMAIN
variables.label: Domain Abbreviation
variables.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

variables.name: LBBLFL
variables.label: Baseline Flag
variables.description: Indicator for whether the result is a baseline value

variables.name: LBCAT
variables.label: Category for Lab Test
variables.description: Used to define a category of lab tests (e.g., HEMATOLOGY, CHEMISTRY)

variables.name: LBDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of Specimen Collection
variables.description: Date/time of specimen collection in ISO 8601

variables.name: LBDY
variables.label: Study Day of Specimen Collection
variables.description: Study day of specimen collection

variables.name: LBFAST
variables.label: Fasting Status
variables.description: Was the subject fasting for this specimen collection?

variables.name: LBMETHOD
variables.label: Method of Test or Examination
variables.description: Method of the laboratory test

variables.name: LBNRIND
variables.label: Reference Range Indicator
variables.description: Indicates where the value falls relative to the reference range (NORMAL, LOW, HIGH)

variables.name: LBORNRHI
variables.label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Orig Unit
variables.description: Upper limit of the reference range in original units

variables.name: LBORNRLO
variables.label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Orig Unit
variables.description: Lower limit of the reference range in original units

variables.name: LBORRES
variables.label: Result or Finding in Original Units
variables.description: Result of the lab test as originally received

variables.name: LBORRESU
variables.label: Original Units
variables.description: Original units for LBORRES

variables.name: LBSCAT
variables.label: Subcategory for Lab Test
variables.description: Subcategory for the lab test

variables.name: LBSEQ
variables.label: Sequence Number
variables.description: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness

variables.name: LBSPEC
variables.label: Specimen Type
variables.description: Type of specimen (e.g., BLOOD, SERUM, URINE, PLASMA)

variables.name: LBSTNRHI
variables.label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Std Units
variables.description: Upper limit of the reference range in standard units

variables.name: LBSTNRLO
variables.label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Std Units
variables.description: Lower limit of the reference range in standard units

variables.name: LBSTRESC
variables.label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
variables.description: Contains the result value for all findings in character format

variables.name: LBSTRESN
variables.label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
variables.description: Numeric result value in standard units

variables.name: LBSTRESU
variables.label: Standard Units
variables.description: Standardized units for LBSTRESN

variables.name: LBTEST
variables.label: Lab Test or Examination Name
variables.description: Verbatim name of the test (e.g., Alanine Aminotransferase)

variables.name: LBTESTCD
variables.label: Lab Test or Examination Short Name
variables.description: Short name of the lab test (e.g., ALT, AST, CREAT)

variables.name: STUDYID
variables.label: Study Identifier
variables.description: Unique identifier for a study

variables.name: USUBJID
variables.label: Unique Subject Identifier
variables.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies

variables.name: VISIT
variables.label: Visit Name
variables.description: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter

variables.name: VISITNUM
variables.label: Visit Number
variables.description: Numeric version of VISIT

related_domains.description: Abnormal lab values (LBNRIND = HIGH/LOW) may correlate with or trigger adverse events
Each lab result in LB links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Laboratory tests are often scheduled relative to treatment exposure to monitor drug effects

VS.name: Vital Signs
VS.description: One record per vital sign measurement per time point per subject.
VS.structure: One record per vital sign per time point per subject

variables.name: DOMAIN
variables.label: Domain Abbreviation
variables.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

variables.name: STUDYID
variables.label: Study Identifier
variables.description: Unique identifier for a study

variables.name: USUBJID
variables.label: Unique Subject Identifier
variables.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies

variables.name: VISIT
variables.label: Visit Name
variables.description: Protocol-defined name of encounter

variables.name: VISITNUM
variables.label: Visit Number
variables.description: Numeric visit

variables.name: VSBLFL
variables.label: Baseline Flag
variables.description: About whether this is a baseline value

variables.name: VSCAT
variables.label: Category of Vital Signs
variables.description: Category of vital signs test

variables.name: VSDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of Measurements
variables.description: Date/time in ISO 8601

variables.name: VSDY
variables.label: Study Day of Vital Signs
variables.description: Study day of vital signs measurement

variables.name: VSLOC
variables.label: Location of Vital Signs Measurement
variables.description: Location on body used for measurement

variables.name: VSORRES
variables.label: Result or Finding in Original Units
variables.description: Result of the vital signs measurement as originally collected

variables.name: VSORRESU
variables.label: Original Units
variables.description: Units for VSORRES

variables.name: VSPOS
variables.label: Vital Signs Position of Subject
variables.description: Position of the subject during observations (e.g., STANDING, SITTING, SUPINE)

variables.name: VSSEQ
variables.label: Sequence Number
variables.description: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness

variables.name: VSSTAT
variables.label: Completion Status
variables.description: Used to indicate when a test was not done (NOT DONE)

variables.name: VSSTRESC
variables.label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
variables.description: Character result in standard format

variables.name: VSSTRESN
variables.label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
variables.description: Numeric result/finding in standard units

variables.name: VSSTRESU
variables.label: Standard Units
variables.description: Standardized units

variables.name: VSTEST
variables.label: Vital Signs Test Name
variables.description: Verbatim name of the vital signs test

variables.name: VSTESTCD
variables.label: Vital Signs Test Short Name
variables.description: Short name (e.g., SYSBP, DIABP, PULSE, TEMP, WEIGHT, HEIGHT)

related_domains.description: Each vital sign measurement in VS links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Vital signs are often measured relative to dosing events to monitor treatment impact

standards_flow.description: CDASH (Clinical Data Acquisition Standards Harmonization) defines collection standards that map to SDTM tabulation domains
SDTM tabulation datasets are transformed into ADaM analysis datasets for statistical analysis
ADaM datasets are used to produce Tables, Listings, and Figures for regulatory submissions
